onboarding.question: While specifying the onboarding engineering spec, I found that AC 10 uses "a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action" without defining what "clear" means in implementable terms. This requires the product spec to be revised to replace "clear" with "a tappable button — not a text link — labeled to sign up or sign-in, visible without scrolling in the context where it appears."
onboarding.recommendations:
1. My recommendation: Accept the revision. Replace "a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action" in AC 10 with "a tappable button — not a text link — labeled to sign in or sign up, visible without scrolling in the context where it appears."
→ Rationale: A button (not a text link) is the minimum bar for discoverability at a moment when we are actively trying to convert a logged-out user — and "visible without scrolling" is the only way to guarantee the CTA lands, since the nudge appears inline in a conversation thread that may already have content above it.

Applying the patch to AC 10 now.

---

**Updated AC 10:**

*Before:* "...rendered as a conversational system message with a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action..."

*After:* "...rendered as a conversational system message with a tappable button — not a text link — labeled to sign in or sign up, visible without scrolling in the context where it appears..."
